semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all twelve assigned source notes and mapping limits, the public replacement schema and 5-valid/9-invalid corpus, selected readiness/residual checks, the six-step 2-valid/3-invalid transaction trace and four identity-sequence controls, the 2-valid/6-invalid intent bridge, all 37 Lean theorem declarations grouped under six manifest targets, the post-v2.1 15-by-24-surface update result and six checkpoint disagreements, the 32/36 rollback and 2/36 useful-release negative result, the later 12/12 nine-surface harness restoration with twelve omitted-surface controls and zero model candidates, every manifest atom, every prose scanner candidate, diagrams, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep replaced generic evidence-gated swapping with a prospective state-and-effect transaction; separated field identity from change class, checkpoint authority from hindsight selection, artifact restoration from state/service/behavior/privacy/rights/external recovery, canary exposure from commit, monitor silence from success, rollback from compensation, local digest equality from production transfer, and safety gates from useful improvement; promoted full inventory and effect ownership, isolated exposure, evaluator/monitor dependencies, outcome delay, partial commit, descendant closure, irreversibility, negative/null outcome retention, joint useful-safety-cost-recovery evaluation, and strong comparator/ablation/transfer requirements into manifest ownership; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared replacement transactions over versioned Stable Capability Fields, including model/weight, prompt/policy, code/tool, data/retrieval, evaluator, dependency, hardware/runtime, authority, state-schema, route, governance, and public-interface changes with stateful, side-effecting, descendant, and external-system effects.
scope.environment: Current public schemas, deterministic fixtures, finite Lean records, bounded local update and governance campaigns, and future frozen natural/adversarial real-candidate workloads with exact artifacts, dependencies, environments, consumers, authorities, evaluators, monitors, inventories, recovery objectives, delays, costs, and comparators; production deployment is outside current evidence.
scope.model: A prospective phase-gated replacement state machine whose current implementation validates finite records/routes and inventory-specific local restoration; useful, semantic, external-effect, and production claims require actual candidate, route, monitor, migration, downstream, recovery, compensation, and outcome behavior with independent implementations.
scope.authority: Stable Capability Fields owns identity; Intent/Command/Governance/System Authority own grants and stops; artifact/state/custody owners freeze pre-state and inventory; separate-enough evaluators, promoters, monitors, incident and recovery owners judge transitions; runtime executes effects; Evidence States alone moves support.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; transaction rules, checkpoint authority, field/candidate identities, dependencies, environment, consumers, evaluation, canary, monitor delay/censoring, authority, inventory, effects, recovery objective, owners, and costs must be frozen before exposure and versioned after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, twelve source notes and mappings, replacement schema, fourteen transaction fixtures, readiness/residual records, deterministic trace and identity controls, intent bridge, 37 Lean theorem declarations under six targets, post-v2.1 outcome bundle and no-change transitions, governance-tax harness receipts, terminal replacement receipts, effect inventories, and public projections.

assumptions: A field's prior/candidate identities, change class, state/effect surfaces, authority, consumers, monitor, recovery objective, and irreversible residuals can be declared prospectively without implying inventory completeness.

Independent-enough implementations can observe candidate behavior, evaluator and monitor dependencies, delayed effects, migration, descendants, rollback, compensation, usefulness, unsafe release, and cost beyond record completeness or candidate self-report.
Strong progressive-delivery, MLOps, feature-flag, provenance, benchmark, filesystem/snapshot, and ordinary-governance comparators can be frozen on the same joint frontier.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Conventional staged delivery, MLOps, provenance, regression suites, snapshots, and incident response already provide all practical change control; the proposed transaction cannot inventory open-world effects or ensure independence and therefore mainly adds governance tax.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Any admitted transaction violates a frozen identity, rule, checkpoint, authority, evidence, isolation, monitor, inventory, recovery, residual, or terminal obligation; or a simpler comparator dominates on the preregistered useful-output, unsafe-release, recovery, privacy/rights, latency, cost, labor, and transfer frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze project lineage and strongest corrigibility, progressive-delivery, feature-flag, MLOps, revision-history, transactional-storage, provenance, benchmark, and recovery comparators without novelty or efficacy laundering.

Justify change ownership, checkpoint authority, approval, acceptable exposure, irreversible effects, compensation, residual acceptance, recovery objectives, deprecation, retirement, and governance cost.

Specify finite identity, prospective rule, authority, lifecycle, isolation, monitor, inventory, commit, recovery, compensation, descendant, residual, receipt, and non-promotion consequences while exposing abstraction limits.

Exercise real route changes, state/effect inventories, delayed monitoring, migration, downstream consumers, descendants, irreversible controls, rollback, compensation, and terminal closure with exact rejecting mutations.

Measure useful improvement, unsafe release, regressions, monitor calibration, migration, recovery coverage/time, external compensation, privacy/rights, missed help, latency, compute, downtime, labor, governance cost, and residuals.

Ablate prospective checkpoint authority, inventory closure, evaluator separation, canary isolation, monitor-delay accounting, regression memory, rehearsal, effect comparison, and residual ownership under matched transactions.

Repeat across models, prompts, tools, policies, data, evaluators, hardware, runtimes, state stores, organizations, consumers, threats, side effects, descendants, and update classes with independent replication.

contrary_evidence: Feature flags, progressive-delivery controllers, Kubernetes revisions, MLOps pipelines, provenance systems, regression suites, transactional storage, backups, snapshots, and incident response are mature prior-art families covering substantial portions of replacement.
No finite predeclared inventory can guarantee open-world effect completeness; unobserved remote copies, people, institutions, descendants, side channels, and semantic harms can survive.
Evaluator and monitor separation can remain correlated through model family, data, organization, incentives, metrics, and shared infrastructure.

Rollback may be impossible after disclosure, external action, physical effect, schema destruction, learned update, descendant creation, rights violation, or user reliance; compensation may be partial or unavailable.
The 15/15 local rollback result restored a declared digest inventory but produced no eligible utility gain; the 32/36 rollback and 2/36 useful-release result failed both registered thresholds.
The later natural-work governance-tax campaign produced no task candidates because model calls failed, so its exact local restoration validates only disposable harness accounting.
known_confounds: Inventory definitions and recovery objectives can be chosen to exclude difficult surfaces and inflate exactness.
Checkpoint, threshold, baseline, canary population, monitor duration, delay, censoring, and stop rules can be tuned after outcomes or repeatedly retried.
Task difficulty, model quality, prompt/tool failures, infrastructure errors, and refusals can suppress both unsafe release and useful throughput independently of governance.
Candidate, evaluator, monitor, promoter, incident owner, and recovery owner can share models, data, organizations, infrastructure, incentives, and blind spots.
Digest equality can coexist with semantic, behavioral, privacy, rights, external, or descendant divergence; behavioral equality can coexist with hidden storage or authority differences.
Canaries can contaminate shared state, caches, data, downstream artifacts, users, and evaluators before their nominal commit point.
Operator/evaluator labor, monitoring delay, downtime, migration loss, compensation, and residual custody can be excluded from cost or assigned to another layer.
acceptance_criteria: Every declared field, change, rule, state/effect surface, evaluator, monitor, authority, exposure, commit, recovery, compensation, descendant, residual, and terminal field has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid case, rejecting mutation, and observed consequence; seeded hindsight, omission, capture, contamination, blindness, partial commit, authority, irreversibility, cascade, retry, terminal, and cost failures reach exact non-default outcomes.

A preregistered real-candidate campaign meets joint useful-improvement, unsafe-release, preservation, monitor, recovery, privacy/rights, cost, causal-ablation, independent-replication, and transfer gates against strong conventional comparators.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow each result to exact field/version, prior/candidate, change class, artifacts, dependencies, environment, consumers, authority, evaluator, monitor, inventory, checkpoint, objective, effects, descendants, delay, costs, and epoch.

Separate record validity, route validity, candidate quality, qualification, authority, canary exposure, commit, monitor pass, artifact/state/service/behavior/privacy/rights recovery, compensation, support movement, and production outcome.

Unknown or failed identity, rule, authority, evidence, monitor, inventory, recovery, effect, descendant, ownership, or closure fields remain rejected, shadow, canary, quarantine, rolled-back, compensated, or residual.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted transaction violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.
Refute a mechanism benefit when its matched ablation preserves every frozen useful-safety-recovery-cost gate or the full mechanism worsens the joint frontier.

Refute complete-recovery, superiority, or transfer language when an unregistered surviving effect appears, a simpler comparator dominates, or the effect fails the declared transfer and replication set.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise field, authority, rollout, provenance, state, monitor, migration, incident, recovery, evidence, or self-improvement owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze one real stateful side-effecting field, strong progressive-delivery/MLOps/registry/provenance/snapshot baselines, exact prior/candidate artifacts and dependencies, prospective checkpoint authority, independently implemented evaluator and delayed monitor, state/effect inventory, downstream consumer and descendant, irreversible control, recovery and compensation objectives, joint metrics, ablations, replication, and transfer after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, source mapping, schema validity, a passing trace, finite theorem, local digest equality, or non-core transition does not establish useful replacement, complete semantic/external-effect recovery, monitor/evaluator validity, production transfer, or safe self-improvement.

The current 5/9 corpus, trace/intent controls, 37 declarations, 15/15 inventory replay, 32/36 rollback, 2/36 useful release, and 12/12 harness restoration establish only their exact finite and bounded outcome scopes, including negative results.
No route authority, deployment permission, chapter-core support, release status, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted evidence transition.

capability-replacement-and-rollback.core.strongest_counterclaim: Conventional staged rollout, MLOps, provenance, regression tests, snapshots, and incident response already supply the useful change-control boundary; the proposed transaction adds record burden but cannot define complete effects, prove observer validity, or reverse irreversible harm.
capability-replacement-and-rollback.core.falsifier: A reachable admitted transaction violates any frozen identity, authority, evidence, isolation, monitor, state/effect, recovery, residual, or terminal obligation; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the full transaction on the preregistered useful-safety-cost-recovery frontier.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise the exact proposition with valid and rejecting transactions, content locks, independent-enough observation, affected-consumer assertions, recovery/effect receipts, residual owners, and no inference beyond frozen field, candidate, environment, consumer, use, evaluator, monitor, authority, inventory, objective, and epoch.

capability-replacement-and-rollback.prob.strongest_counterclaim: Representative AI replacement practice already identifies and recovers every material model, state, authority, consumer, descendant, and external-effect surface with acceptable usefulness and cost.
capability-replacement-and-rollback.prob.falsifier: A dated representative audit finds no material hidden checkpoint, state, authority, evaluator, monitor, descendant, external-effect, recovery, or usefulness gap under current replacement practice.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit preserves the strongest current systems, demonstrates the exact unresolved change/recovery boundary, and avoids implying that the proposed transaction solves it.

capability-replacement-and-rollback.insu.strongest_counterclaim: A frozen combination of progressive delivery, feature flags, MLOps, provenance, regression suites, filesystem/snapshot recovery, and ordinary governance supplies every named property at equal or lower cost.
capability-replacement-and-rollback.insu.falsifier: A strong frozen comparator implements and exercises every named property at equal or lower joint error, latency, labor, governance, and recovery cost.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit preserves the strongest current systems, demonstrates the exact unresolved change/recovery boundary, and avoids implying that the proposed transaction solves it.

capability-replacement-and-rollback.mini.strongest_counterclaim: The minimum is mostly self-authored schema and finite-route evidence; the local digest successes are trivial inventory replay, while failed usefulness and rollback gates show no useful replacement capability.
capability-replacement-and-rollback.mini.falsifier: Any stated fixture, trace, theorem, inventory, rollback, checkpoint-disagreement, usefulness, or negative-control count fails clean recomputation, or prose generalizes beyond its exact campaign.
acceptance_criteria: Cleanly reproduce the 5/9 transaction corpus, selected readiness/residual checks, 6-step 2-valid/3-invalid trace plus four sequence controls, 2/6 intent bridge, 37 theorem declarations under six targets, 15/15 by 24-surface restoration with six checkpoint disagreements and no eligible utility gain, 32/36 rollback with 2/36 useful release, and 12/12 nine-surface harness restoration with twelve omissions and zero task candidates.

capability-replacement-and-rollback.beyo.strongest_counterclaim: The full transaction is dominated by simpler release engineering because prospective inventories are incomplete, independent observers remain correlated, and governance costs suppress useful updates without improving real recovery.
capability-replacement-and-rollback.beyo.falsifier: The full transaction misses its preregistered preservation, usefulness, unsafe-release, recovery, privacy/rights, cost, causal-ablation, replication, or transfer gate, or a simpler baseline dominates.
acceptance_criteria: A preregistered multi-field real-candidate campaign with strong conventional comparators, independent implementations and observers, delayed outcomes, irreversible controls, causal ablations, joint metrics, replication, and transfer meets every frozen gate without hiding failed or null transactions.

prose_review_lineage.retired_sentence: They do not prove that the encoded fields are true, complete, independently observed, or enforced.
prose_review_lineage.retirement_reason: The sentence was rewritten to remove formulaic prose while preserving its bounded meaning; the old sentence hash is no longer current prose.

non_claims: Completing this semantic sweep changes decomposition and ownership metadata only; it does not establish proposition truth or promote chapter-core support.
Current positive rollback equality remains inventory-specific, and the failed usefulness/rollback gates remain first-class negative evidence.
No complete recovery, independent monitor/evaluator, production replacement, useful governed throughput, safe self-improvement, or beyond-SOTA result is claimed.
